exemplar.title: Backflip Basics: A Step-by-step guide for beginners

detailedExemplarData.title: Backflip Basics: A Step-by-step guide for beginners

strengths: Detailed descriptions of strength-training exercises show effective research and content selection
Inclusion of cognitive approaches and suggested steps indicates thoughtful application of self-management strategies
weaknesses: ATL skills are not explicitly identified by name or category
Insufficient evidence linking applied skills to the quality or outcomes of the final product
No discussion of collaboration or feedback cycles to demonstrate communication ATL skills
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student clearly researched and described exercises and cognitive strategies, but needs to name the specific ATL skills used (e.g., research, self-management, communication) and provide concrete examples of how these skills improved the product. Adding evidence of peer feedback or collaborative planning would strengthen this section.

strengths: Clear contents page that demonstrates strong initial organization and provides a useful overview structure for the report
weaknesses: Missing an explicit learning goal and measurable success criteria
Plan outlines topics but lacks sequencing, timeline and details about required resources
No explanation of how the topic connects to personal interests or motivations
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: While the report is well organizedâevidenced by the clear contents pageâit does not establish what the student aims to learn or the criteria by which success will be judged. The planning section needs a detailed timeline, resource list and a clear connection to personal goals to meet the IB expectations for a comprehensive plan.

strengths: Conclusion conveys a motivational tone and reflects a positive mindset and resilience
weaknesses: No discussion of the impact of the process on the studentâs own learning or skill development
Lacks evaluation of the guide against defined success criteria with supporting examples
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Although the conclusion shows an encouraging attitude, the reflection is incomplete without an analysis of personal growth or learning gains and a thorough evaluation of the product against success criteria. Incorporating specific evidence of what was learned and how each criterion was met will deepen the reflection.

annotations.comment: Your detailed description of strength-training exercises reflects effective research and selection of relevant content for skill development.
Including cognitive approaches and suggested steps demonstrates thought given to self-management strategies in your learning process.
The clear contents page demonstrates strong initial organization and provides a useful overview structure for your report.
The report lacks an explicit learning goal and measurable success criteria. Define what you aim to learn and how you will know youâve succeeded.
Your plan outlines topics but omits sequencing, timeline and resource details. Include a step-by-step schedule and needed materials.
Thereâs no connection to your personal interests or motivation. Explain how this topic links to your own goals to strengthen planning.
You havenât discussed the impact of this process on your own learning or skill growth. Include evidence of what you learned and how you grew.
The text does not specify which ATL skills you applied. Explicitly identify skills (e.g., research, self-management) and explain their role.
Thereâs little evidence linking skill application to the quality of your product. Provide examples showing how particular skills improved outcomes.
Consider describing how collaboration or feedback cycles supported your learning, demonstrating communication ATL skills.
The conclusionâs motivational tone shows awareness of mindset and resilience, which is a positive reflection on your learning attitude.
Thereâs no evaluation of your guide against defined success criteria. Reflect on whether each criterion was met, using specific examples.
